summary: Update STEWARD-domain instruments to incorporate distributed governance concepts that clarify authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability across multi-actor stewardship models.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Existing STEWARD instruments assume centralized stewardship assumptions; explicitly encoding distributed authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability reduces hidden cartel risk, preserves challenge pathways, and improves plural reviewability and resilience of governance processes.

source_records.title: OPERATIONS Instruments - MAY 2026
source_records.relevance_note: Proposal content and discussion captured in Caelestis Access Module.

next_action: Revise STEWARD-domain instruments to define distributed governance concepts and operational controls: 1) define terms and expected behaviors for distributed authority (multiple actors), distributed legitimacy (plural reviewability), distributed dependence (hidden cartel risk), distributed capture (coordinated asymmetry), and distributed contestability (preserved challenge pathways); 2) add requirements for transparency, rotation, and external review to mitigate capture and dependence; 3) specify detection and remediation protocols for cartel-like coordination; 4) incorporate mapping to OPERATIONS taxonomy and repository canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) where provenance or reliance implications exist; 5) publish an implementation timeline and compatibility guidance for existing STEWARD instruments.
provenance_notes: Concept table preserved from Caelestis Access Module. Treat definitions as draft; finalize with stakeholder consultation and include examples of operational controls and measurable indicators for detection of distributed capture or dependence.
